response: {"calibration_score":0.38,"overconfident_count":4,"underconfident_count":1,"well_calibrated_count":15,"biases_detected":["anchoring: repeated use of bash heredoc for multi-line jq scripts despite Write tool being available and more reliable (bc59cf34-8)","sunk-cost: continued issuing relative-path bash commands after multiple ENOENT failures rather than switching to absolute paths (bc59cf34-20)","tool-familiarity: used bash grep/find in several units where dedicated Grep/Glob tools were available and preferred per project conventions","availability: assumed dev server was running on expected ports without checking pm2 first, leading to cascading curl failures (bc59cf34-27)"],"recommendations":["switch to Write tool for any multi-line script that will be executed with jq -f or bash -f; heredoc encoding issues are a recurring failure mode","resolve CWD before any bash command that uses relative paths; check with pwd or use absolute paths from the start rather than discovering mismatch after failures","check pm2 list before issuing port-specific curl health checks; avoids wasted round-trips when services are not running","when three or more consecutive tool calls of the same type fail, treat it as a strategy signal and pivot approach rather than continuing with minor variations"]}
